questions.question: What does EAGLE-3 do that ngram drafts do not?
options: It trains draft heads on the target model's hidden states for higher acceptance rates
questions.explanation: EAGLE-3 uses a draft model (small, fast) to generate multiple candidate tokens in parallel, then verifies them with the target model (large, accurate), achieving 2-3x speedup.

questions.question: Why is p99 tail latency reported across batch sizes 1, 8, and 32?
options: Steady-state tokens-per-second can hide that the verify pass on rejection is more expensive than vanilla decoding
vLLM cannot serve batch sizes below 8
p99 only matters at batch size 32
Tail latency is independent of batch size
questions.explanation: The server streams verified tokens to clients in chunks, reducing round-trip latency and enabling incremental processing for real-time applications.

questions.question: Why does acceptance rate drift when the traffic distribution shifts?
options: Draft alignment depends on the input distribution: ShareGPT, code, and domain data exercise different patterns
The OS scheduler reshuffles GPU memory
questions.explanation: Token consistency checks compare draft model predictions with target model verification, rejecting divergent tokens and triggering fallback to direct generation.

questions.question: Which Kubernetes HPA signal does the deployment scale on?
questions.explanation: Model routing directs simple queries to the draft model for low-latency responses and complex queries to the full model for higher accuracy, based on query complexity heuristics.

questions.question: What does P-EAGLE add over serial EAGLE-3?
options: Parallel speculation across a tree of draft branches verified in one target pass
questions.explanation: The capstone measures speedup (tokens/sec vs baseline), accuracy preservation (perplexity of generated text), and fallback rate (percentage of queries requiring direct generation).

questions.question: Which throughput target does the rubric demand against the non-speculative baseline?
options: At least 2.5x at matched quality on two models
questions.explanation: Server stability is tested with load tests (concurrent request patterns), memory stress tests (large context windows), and failure recovery (graceful degradation on model errors).
